epp:phrases:
REF2029 OA Compliance
Deposit Exceptions 8.1.1. The following exceptions apply where the output is unable to meet the deposit requirements. In these cases, the output will not be required to meet any of the open access criteria (deposit, discovery, access or licensing). It would be unlawful to deposit, or request the deposit of, the output or to otherwise make this available. The publication actively disallows open access deposit in a repository and was the most appropriate publication for the output. Subject to delay in securing the final peer-reviewed text for deposit – for instance, where a paper has multiple authors. Deposit Exceptions Reason Please provide supporting information for the exception:
Access Exceptions 8.2.1. The following exceptions apply where deposit of the output is possible, but there are issues to do with meeting one or more of accessibility, embargo and/or licensing requirements. Outputs will be exempt from the relevant requirements, but still be required to meet all other open access policy requirements. The publication requires an embargo period that exceeds the stated maxima and was the most appropriate publication for the output. Outputs will not need to meet the embargo requirements in this case. Third-party content was included for which licences could not be obtained at a reasonable cost. The output will not need to meet access and/or licensing requirements (as relevant) in this case. Access Exceptions Reason Please provide supporting information for the exception:
Technical Exceptions 8.3.1 The following exceptions deal with cases where an output is unable to meet the criteria due to a technical issue. In the following cases, the output will not be required to meet the open access criteria (deposit, discovery, access or licensing). Output has a demonstrable and substantive connection to the submitting institution but was published following the end of the author’s period of employment, and it has not been possible to determine compliance with the criteria. Outputs were authored in whole by one or more staff who are or were ineligible to be included in the volume measure for REF 2029. The funding bodies have agreed this exception across the publication period for REF 2029 in order to support submission of a wide range of eligible outputs. Technical Exceptions Reason Please provide supporting information for the exception:
Further (‘Other’) Exception 8.4.1. “Other” exception should be used where criteria beyond the control of the higher education institution (HEI) (including but not limited to: personal circumstances of the author, industrial action, closure days and software issues) prevent compliance. If ‘other’ exception is selected, the output will not need to meet the open access criteria (deposit, discovery, access or licensing requirements). Further (‘Other’) Exception Reason Please provide supporting information for the exception:
License exception due to publication agreement 7.5.4. In-scope outputs shared by deposit (7.2.3.) do not need to meet the licensing standards set out in 7.5.2. if the deposited output (AAM) is subject to terms of a publication agreement which limits sharing and/or use of downloaded materials. These outputs will still be required to meet all other conditions for deposit, discovery and access. Application of an exception will not be required.
Gold/Diamond/Platinum OA 7.2.1. Published open access: Where an output is made fully open access on publication (commonly termed ’Gold‘ open access, also ’Diamond‘ or ’Platinum‘), conditional to it also fully meeting discovery, access and licensing criteria there is no further action required.
Compliant elsewhere This record meets OA compliance criteria in another repository. Compliant elsewhere evidence Please provide a link or description that evidences where this record is otherwise OA compliant.
Override Compliance Checks Prevent REF OA compliance checls from being applied, exempting this record.
Date of first compliant Open Access (with valid license) Earliest embargo date
This item is compliant with the Open Access policy
Reason: "
Manual override Flagged as compliant in another platform Open Access with 'Gold', 'Diamond' or 'Platinum' routes (see 7.2.1) Deposit exception applied (see 8.1) Meets compliance rules with embargo exception (see 8.2.2)
The output will be compliant if it is made open access within the allowed timeframe (when the embargo releases or within three months of publication date). The output will be compliant if it is made open access within the allowed timeframe (when the embargo releases or within three months of publication date). An embargo exception has been applied (see 8.2.2).
Meets compliance rules with license exception (see 8.2.3) Technical exception technical (see 8.3) Further exception applied (see 8.4) All compliance rules met!
For more information about OA compliance, please see the REF 2029 Open Access Policy.
To be compliant, an item must meet at least one of the following conditions:
The deposit, discovery and access requirements are met.
There is a deposit exception.
There is an access exception and the deposit and discovery requirements are met.
There is a technical exception.
There is a 'further' exception.
To be deposit compliant, all of the following conditions must be met.
The output must have been deposited as the author’s accepted and final peer reviewed text.
7.2.5. The deposited output should be either the Author Accepted Manuscript (AAM) or where permitted under publication agreement, the final Version of Record (VOR).
The Date of first compliant deposit must be set (ie. the item is a compliant deposit).
The output must have been deposited as soon after the date of publication as possible.
7.2.3. Outputs shared by deposit: Where an output will be compliant via deposit in a suitable repository or other appropriate platform, a copy of such output should be deposited within three months of publication. The must be no later than three months after the .
To be discovery compliant, all of the following conditions must be met.
The output must be discoverable by readers and automated tools such as search engines.
7.3.2. Discovery requirements are typically fulfilled through storage and open presentation of a bibliographic or metadata record on a publisher/publication site, repository or other suitable platform.
The item must be in the
To be access compliant, all of the following conditions must be met.
The output must meet the access requirements as soon as possible.
7.4.2. The output must be made fully accessible on deposit, subject to the expiry of any required embargo period.
The output must be open, unless an embargo applies in which case it must be made open as soon as the embargo expires.
The output must be made accessible using an open licence.
7.5.2. Outputs should be shared as openly as possible; the funding bodies’ strong preference is for licensing as CC-BY or other licence formats meeting this standard of openness. However, licensing outputs at CC-BY-NC or CC-BY-ND (including CC-BY-NC-ND) or licences meeting an equivalent standard of openness are also permitted.
The licence attached to the AAM or VoR must be one of CC-BY, CC-BY-NC or CC-BY-ND.
Embargo periods should not exceed the following maxima.
7.6.4. Embargo periods should not exceed the following maxima:
6 months for Main Panel A and Main Panel B.
12 months for Main Panel C and Main Panel D.
The must not exceed the stated maxima for the target .
REF 2029 Compliance Outputs published between Jan 26 to Dec 28 Outputs published between Jan 21 to Dec 25 Outputs published between Jan 21 to Dec 25
EPrint Fields REF2029 Compliance Fields
